text: Increased plantarflexion range of motion is a risk factor for plantar fasciitis in physically active individuals, with those affected showing approximately 7 degrees greater plantarflexion ROM.
Higher body mass index is a risk factor for plantar fasciitis, with affected individuals having approximately 2.1 kg/m² higher BMI than unaffected individuals.
Greater body mass is a risk factor for plantar fasciitis, with affected individuals weighing approximately 4.5 kg more than unaffected individuals.

Plantar fasciitis prevention and treatment should target modifiable risk factors including excessive plantarflexion range of motion and elevated body mass, which increase load on plantar surface structures.
Body mass index shows the most homogeneous association with plantar fasciitis risk across studies, with no between-study heterogeneity.
